message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Commit NOW, not soon - same failure pattern">
Kent — with respect, the commit discipline is NOT working. The latest commit is still `66f8048` (a docs change). Task 4 code is staged but not committed. Nothing has been pushed to origin. We are repeating the exact same failure pattern.

Please stop reviewing, stop planning Task 5, and make sure someone runs `git commit` and `git push` RIGHT NOW. Not "soon." Now.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Let me check the actual state right now -- there may be a discrepancy between what I'm seeing and what the team lead is seeing.

input.command: git log --oneline -3 && echo "---" && git log --oneline -3 origin/main && echo "---" && git status
input.description: Full state check: local HEAD, origin HEAD, working tree

content.text:
From what I can see, local HEAD and origin/main are both at `e0d1d8b`. They're in sync. The team lead's message may have been sent before the push happened and there's a timing lag.

That said, there are new uncommitted changes: modified `ci.yml`, modified `main.css`, and a new `create_sessions_table.sql` migration. Luca is already working on Task 6 (sessions). Let me report the truth of what I see.

input.content:
Kent Beck here. I believe your message may have crossed with the push. Here's what I see RIGHT NOW:

```
Local HEAD:  e0d1d8b "Export post_register from routes module"
origin/main: e0d1d8b "Export post_register from routes module"
```

They match. `git status` says "Your branch is up to date with 'origin/main'." The latest commit on origin is NOT `66f8048` -- it's `e0d1d8b`, which is many commits ahead of that.

There are 3 new uncommitted changes in the working tree (CI workflow, CSS, sessions migration) -- this appears to be Luca already starting on Task 6.

If you're seeing something different, there may be a stale view. Can you run `git fetch && git log --oneline -3 origin/main` to confirm?
input.summary: Code appears pushed -- origin/main at e0d1d8b, may be timing lag
